images.caption: Figure 1:Difference Between Self-Flow and SRA.Self-Flow adopts SRA’s Self-Representation Alignment method while differs in the student input processing: SRA utilizes a single noise level (t1t_{1}) for all student input tokens, whereas Self-Flow employs a dual-timestep scheduler where tokens at two distinct noise levels (t1t_{1}andt2t_{2}) coexist in the same image.

Figure 2:Attention Separation disentangles self-supervision from augmentation.Given a sample with dual-timestep scheduling, Attention Separation preserves the heterogeneous-noise input but partitions attention into independent timestep groups, so tokens att1t_{1}cannot interact with tokens att2t_{2}. This removes token interactions while keeping the noise-state augmentation introduced by dual-timestep scheduling. Meanwhile, Attention Separation can also be interpreted as creating multiple part-conditioned views of one image to expand the training distribution, thereby also acting as a data augmentation.

Figure 3:Attention Separation Visualization.Given a dual-timestep input, full attention allows all tokens to attend to each other regardless of their assigned timestep. In contrast, Attention Separation applies a block-diagonal attention mask: tokens from the same timestep group can interact, while tokens from different timestep groups are blocked.

Figure 4:Comparison between single-timestep and dual-timestep training under matched attention settings.Left: without Attention Separation.Right: with Attention Separation. Dual-timestep training consistently improves over single-timestep training under both full attention and attention-separation, supporting the view that its benefit comes from noise-state augmentation.

header: 6Attention Separation Is Also a Data Augmentation

images.caption: Figure 5:Effect of adding full-image samples. We compare Attention Separation applied to all dual-timestep samples with a mixed setting that includes full-image single-timestep samples. Adding full-image samples substantially reduces the mismatch caused by strong separation.
Figure 6:Qualitative results on ImageNet using SiT-XL + ours. We use classifier-free guidance withww= 4.0.
